page: Vultr vhf-12c-48gb is a virtual server within the High Frequency Compute family, featuring 12 vCPUs and 12 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. The system is powered by an Intel Core Processor (Skylake, IBRS, no TSX) running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. It is equipped with 48.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled as a 768 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU hardware is present on this server configuration.

Benchmark data reveals a mixture of performance tiers. Single-core CPU performance is strong, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2435.37 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 41.11 GB/sec, which ranks in the top tier. Multi-core performance is average, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 17894.4 and a PassMark CPU score of 17761.33. Memory performance shows a significant contrast: the PassMark memory score is in the top tier, and cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 865.54 GB/sec, but uncached large-block read and write bandwidths are weak, measuring 33.97 GB/sec and 18.41 GB/sec respectively.

This server is configured for workloads requiring a balance of compute and local storage. Database operations and Redis SET operations both perform in the average tier, with Redis achieving 13.71 million operations per second. Large-model LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with the 7B model prompt processing and text generation falling into the weak category. Consequently, this server is suited for general-purpose web serving, single-threaded compression tasks, and moderate database workloads, while being less optimal for memory-intensive uncached streaming or large-scale AI inference.
description: The Vultr vhf-12c-48gb is an x86_64 virtual server in the High Frequency Compute family, configured with 12 shared vCPUs, 48.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 768 GB NVMe SSD. Powered by a 2.0 GHz Intel Skylake processor, it delivers strong single-core performance, highlighted by a top-tier single-threaded gzip compression speed of 41.11 GB/sec and a strong stress-ng single-core score. However, multi-core operations and uncached memory bandwidth benchmarks are average to weak, with large-block write bandwidth limited to 18.41 GB/sec. The server is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and moderate database operations, but exhibits performance tradeoffs in memory-intensive tasks and larger LLM inference workloads.
og_description: Vultr vhf-12c-48gb is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 12 shared Intel vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and 768 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance alongside average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-12c-48gb is an x86_64 server with 12 shared vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and 768 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A shared-core virtual server balancing strong single-threaded processing and local NVMe storage for general-purpose and moderate database workloads.
bullet_points: 12 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs and 48.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 768 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and top-tier single-threaded gzip compression performance
Average multi-core CPU and database benchmark results
Weak uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidth
Best suited for general-purpose web serving and moderate database applications
